reasoning: The SOC2 control CC7.3 focuses on evaluating security events and taking action to prevent or address failures. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logging is enabled and properly configured to capture security events. Additionally, admission controllers can be configured to detect and respond to security events at the time of resource creation or modification. NetworkPolicies can be used to monitor and control network traffic, which is crucial for detecting potential security incidents. These mappings ensure that Kubernetes clusters can evaluate and respond to security events effectively.
title: CC7.3 The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures
